word: G373 - ἀναπαύω - anapaúō - an-ap-ow'-o - Take ease

sections.paragraph:
- Original: ἀναπαύω

- Transliteration: Anapauo

- Phonetic: an-ap-ow'-o

- Definition:

1. to cause or permit one to cease from any movement or labour in order to recover and collect his strength

2. to give rest, refresh, to give one's self rest, take rest

3. to keep quiet, of calm and patient expectation

- Origin: from G303 and G3973

- TDNT entry: 06:50,6

- Part(s) of speech: Verb

- Strong's: From G303 and G3973; (reflexively) to repose (literally or figuratively (be exempt) remain); by implication to refresh: - take ease refresh (give take) rest.
